errors.invalidWorldName: Invalid world name — use only letters, numbers, _ and -

editor.ctrlSaveSearch: Ctrl+S to save · Ctrl+F to search

items.q: How do my friends join the server?
items.a: In Valheim, use Join via IP and enter YOUR_IP:2456 (the default port is 2456). Then enter the server password. The current address appears on the Overview tab, in the “How to connect” block.

items.q: Where do I set the server name and password?
items.a: On the Server tab. The password must be at least 5 characters and cannot contain the server name. Save and restart to apply.

items.q: The server doesn't show up in the public list. What now?
items.a: Valheim's public list often takes a few minutes and sometimes fails. Prefer Join via IP. Also confirm that SERVER_PUBLIC is set to true on the Server tab.

items.q: Do I need to open ports on my router?
items.a: Yes — to play over the internet, forward UDP ports 2456–2458 to the server machine (port forwarding).

items.q: How do I enable crossplay (PC + Xbox/Game Pass)?
items.a: On the Server tab, add -crossplay in the Extra arguments field and restart.

items.q: What's the difference between Start, Stop, Restart, Pause, and Resume?
items.a: Start/Stop/Restart turn the entire container on/off. Pause/Resume only suspend the Valheim process inside the container (faster, keeps the container running).

items.q: What are the Administrators, Banned, and Permitted lists?
items.a: Steam ID lists. Admin gets moderation commands; Banned players cannot join; Permitted works as a whitelist (if filled, only those IDs can join). On Overview, use the Actions menu next to each connected player to promote, kick, or ban without editing files manually.

items.q: How do I use the panel's interactive console?
items.a: The ValheimRcon mod is built into the panel (Integrated badge on the Mods tab). In Modded (BepInEx) mode, the RCON password is generated automatically on first setup. Console and moderation require BepInEx active and the mod enabled.

items.q: How do I change the RCON password?
items.a: Edit config/bepinex/org.tristan.rcon.cfg (the Password field) on Mods → ValheimRcon config, or set PANEL_RCON_PASSWORD in .env. Restart the server after changing.

items.q: What's the difference between kick and ban?
items.a: Kick disconnects the player immediately, but they can rejoin. Ban blocks the Steam ID on the ban list until you unban them. Both require ValheimRcon enabled and an RCON password configured.

items.q: Can I remove ValheimRcon?
items.a: No — it is integrated into the panel and cannot be removed. You can disable it on the Mods tab; when you switch back to Modded (BepInEx) mode, it is re-enabled automatically.

items.q: How do I find a player's Steam ID?
items.a: Connected players appear on Overview with name and Steam ID. For offline players, use steamid.io and copy the steamID64 (17 digits).

items.q: How do I create a new world?
items.a: On the Worlds tab, enter a name and click Create (stays pending) or Create & Activate (switches the server to it). A world is only actually generated on first boot.

items.q: What are the presets (Easy, Hard, Hardcore...)?
items.a: They are the same modifiers as Valheim's world creation screen, saved in the .fwl file. You can use a preset and still override individual settings (combat, resources, raids, portals, death penalty).

items.q: Can I import a world I already have?
items.a: Yes. Copy WorldName.fwl and WorldName.db to config/worlds_local/ (Files tab or Docker volume) and it will appear in the list.

items.q: Does switching worlds delete the previous one?
items.a: No. Switching only changes which world is active; progress on other worlds remains saved in config/worlds_local/.

items.q: How do I install a mod?
items.a: On Mods & Config, paste a Thunderstore URL (page, download link, or r2modman) and click Install, or upload a .zip/.dll.

items.q: Do players need the mod too?
items.a: It depends on the mod. Server-side mods (e.g. ServerSideMap) run only on the server; most gameplay/UI mods must be installed on each player's client at the same version.

items.q: What is BepInEx?
items.a: It is the mod loader used by Valheim. Each mod usually generates a .cfg file in config/bepinex, editable on the Mods & Config tab.

items.q: Vanilla or modded?
items.a: On first launch or on Server → Game updates, choose Vanilla (disables BepInEx and all mods) or Modded (enables BepInEx and integrated ValheimRcon). In vanilla mode, use Administrators in Player Lists to be admin in-game.

items.q: How do game updates work?
items.a: The container uses valheim-updater (SteamCMD). On the Server tab you can enable auto-update, disable it, or click Check for updates now. With mods installed, prefer updating manually after checking compatibility.

items.q: Can updates break mods?
items.a: Yes. A Valheim patch may require new mod versions. Back up, update the game, then use Check for updates on each Thunderstore-linked mod.

items.q: How do I update a mod?
items.a: Mods installed via Thunderstore show version status. Use Check for updates and, if a new version is available, Update mod. Uploaded mods must be linked to a Thunderstore URL for automatic checks.

items.q: I enabled/disabled a mod and nothing changed.
items.a: Mod changes require a server restart. Use the Restart button on Overview.

items.q: Are backups automatic?
items.a: Yes. On the Backups tab, under Automatic schedule, you set the interval. The container copies worlds_local/ to config/backups/ as worlds-*.zip files. Retention: 30 days.

items.q: How do I back up right now?
items.a: Click Create manual backup and choose the type: Active world (quick), Full, or Configs only.

items.q: How do I restore a backup?
items.a: In the backup list, click Restore to here on the desired point. The panel creates a checkpoint, restores files, and restarts the server. Use Restore to latest or Undo last restore to go back.

items.q: What is Run scheduled now?
items.a: Manually triggers the same backup job that runs on the configured interval — different from Create manual backup, which lets you choose scope (world, full, or configs).

items.q: Can I limit how much disk backups use?
items.a: Yes. On the Server tab, under Backup disk usage, use the Total backup limit dropdown. Leave it on Unlimited for no cap, or pick a size (1 GB, 2 GB, 10 GB, etc.) and click Save backup limit. Oldest backups are removed automatically when the cap is exceeded.

items.q: What does Clear all backups now do?
items.a: On the Server tab, this irreversibly deletes every backup ZIP in config/backups/, except backups tied to an active restore or undo checkpoint.

items.q: How do I find a specific file quickly?
items.a: On the Files tab, use the search box to filter by file name. Matching files appear in a flat list for fast access.

items.q: What are the file type filters?
items.a: Click chips such as Config, DLLs, Plugins, or Worlds to narrow the tree to common file types — useful when you only need to edit a few configs or mod files.

items.q: How much RAM does the server need?
items.a: A Valheim server typically uses 2–4 GB, increasing with more players/mods. Adjust the cap on the Server tab under Server capacity. Real-time metrics are on Overview.

items.q: How do I set the player limit?
items.a: On the Server tab under Server capacity. Vanilla supports up to 10 players; above that you need a mod (Valheim Plus or MaxPlayerCount). The panel syncs the value to the mod's .cfg if installed.

items.q: Does changing the RAM limit disconnect players?
items.a: Yes — applying a new limit recreates the container and disconnects anyone online. Do this during quiet hours.

items.q: How do I run the panel + server?
items.a: Copy .env.example to .env, adjust values, and run docker compose up -d. The panel is at http://YOUR_IP:8080.

items.q: I'm getting permission errors on folders.
items.a: When running via Docker, the panel and server use the same user (UID/GID 1000) and share volumes, so permission errors should not occur. Confirm that config/ and data/ belong to UID 1000.

items.q: Is it safe to mount docker.sock in the panel?
items.a: The panel needs docker.sock to control the Valheim container. This grants Docker control to the panel container — keep the panel on a private network/behind a proxy with authentication in production.

items.q: How do I enable Discord notifications?
items.a: Open the Discord tab in the sidebar. Create a webhook in Discord (channel → Integrations → Webhooks), paste the URL, enable Discord, choose events, then Save and Send test. Full guide: see the project docs docs/discord-alerts.md.

items.q: Why did a join alert show a Steam ID?
items.a: Valheim logs the Steam ID first; the character name appears a few seconds later. The panel waits briefly and caches the name for the next join.

items.q: Which events can I turn on?
items.a: Server down/up, start/stop/restart, 80% CPU/RAM load, player join/leave, new mod installed, and backup failure.

items.q: Where do I see what's happening?
items.a: Open Console (docker/BepInEx) in the Tools section. The Audit tab shows all actions taken by the panel. Real-time CPU and RAM are on Overview.

items.q: The panel is unresponsive / showing error 500.
items.a: Check Console and Audit. Confirm Docker is running and the valheim-server container exists.

items.q: A change didn't apply.
items.a: Many changes (mods, lists, running world config) only take effect after restarting the server.

_default.label: Game default
_default.desc: No modifiers — vanilla experience, as before the Hildir's Request patch.

easy.label: Easy
easy.desc: Lighter combat (easy damage) and less frequent raids.

normal.label: Normal
normal.desc: Equivalent to game default — all sliders at Normal.

hard.label: Hard
hard.desc: Hard combat and more frequent raids.

hardcore.label: Hardcore
hardcore.desc: Very hard combat, maximum death penalty, frequent raids, hard portals, and no map.

casual.label: Casual
casual.desc: Very easy combat, light death penalty, more resources, no raids, casual portals, per-player events, and passive mobs.

hammer.label: Hammer mode
hammer.desc: Building with no material cost, raids disabled, and passive mobs.

immersive.label: Immersive
immersive.desc: Portals forbidden, fire spreads across the world, and no map/minimap.

_default.label: Use preset
_default.desc: Inherit combat difficulty from the selected preset.

veryeasy.label: Very easy
veryeasy.desc: 125% player damage, 50% enemy damage, enemies 90% speed/size.

easy.label: Easy
easy.desc: 110% player damage, 75% enemy damage, enemies 95% speed/size.

normal.label: Normal
normal.desc: 100% on all combat parameters. Higher chance of high-level enemies on Hard/Very hard.

hard.label: Hard
hard.desc: 85% player damage, 150% enemy damage, enemies 110% speed/size, 120% level-up rate.

veryhard.label: Very hard
veryhard.desc: 70% player damage, 200% enemy damage, enemies 120% speed/size, 140% level-up rate.

_default.label: Use preset
_default.desc: Inherit death penalty from the selected preset.

casual.label: Casual
casual.desc: Equipment kept on death. Inventory dropped. Skill loss: 1%.

veryeasy.label: Very easy
veryeasy.desc: Drop everything on death. Skill loss: 1% (less than Normal).

easy.label: Easy
easy.desc: Drop everything on death. Skill loss: 2.5%.

normal.label: Normal
normal.desc: Drop everything on death. Skill loss: 5%.

hard.label: Hard
hard.desc: Equipment dropped, inventory permanently destroyed. Skill loss: 7.5%.

hardcore.label: Hardcore
hardcore.desc: All items and skills permanently lost on death.

_default.label: Use preset
_default.desc: Inherit resource rate from the selected preset.

muchless.label: Much less
muchless.desc: 50% of normal mob and object drop rate (≈0.5×).

_default.label: Use preset
_default.desc: Inherit raid frequency from the selected preset.

none.label: None
none.desc: EventRate 0 — daytime raids disabled. Night raids may still occur.

muchless.label: Much less
muchless.desc: Interval ~92 min, 10% chance — far fewer raids.

_default.label: Use preset
_default.desc: Inherit portal rules from the selected preset.

casual.label: Casual
casual.desc: TeleportAll — almost everything can go through portals (except tamed animals).

normal.label: Normal
normal.desc: Non-portable items follow default game rules.

hard.label: No boss portals
hard.desc: Portals unavailable while a boss is active in the area.

veryhard.label: No portals
veryhard.desc: No portals allowed in the world.

nobuildcost.label: No build cost
nobuildcost.desc: Building pieces consume no materials. Recipes still need to be discovered.

playerevents.label: Per-player raids
playerevents.desc: Raids based on each player's individual progress, not bosses killed on the server.

fire.label: Fire hazard
fire.desc: Wood can catch fire and fire spreads across the entire world, not just Ashlands.

passivemobs.label: Passive enemies
passivemobs.desc: Enemies do not attack until provoked.

nomap.label: No map
nomap.desc: Map and minimap disabled — navigate by landmarks only.

ui.settingsDesc: Modifiers saved in the .fwl file — equivalent to Valheim's world creation screen.
ui.effectiveDesc: What will be applied to the world after saving (preset + overrides).
ui.modifiersDesc: Leave on «Use preset» to inherit from the preset above, or choose a specific value.
ui.seedHint: Used only when creating the .fwl file for the first time.
ui.backupHint: Automatic backup of the previous .fwl in panel-data/world_fwl_backups/ before each save.
ui.restartWarning: World is running — restart the server after saving to apply .fwl changes.

list.desc: Lists all commands on the server

ban.desc: Bans by name or Steam ID

give.desc: Gives an item to a player

findPlayer.desc: Finds a player and shows details

findObjects.desc: Searches for objects in the world

addGlobalKey.desc: Adds a global key (e.g. boss defeated)

hints.bepinexRequired: RCON console only works with BepInEx active — choose Modded on the Server tab.
hints.modRequired: Enable the ValheimRcon mod on Mods & Config to use console and moderation.
hints.configPending: Waiting for RCON configuration — restart the panel or Valheim server.
hints.serverStopped: Start the server to use the interactive console.

console.placeholder: RCON command (e.g. save, list, kick Name...)
console.inputHints: Tab autocompletes · Enter sends · output appears in the logs above

helpModal.title: RCON commands
helpModal.intro: Click a command to fill the console. Use list on the server to see all installed commands.

setup.title: Set up server
setup.subtitle: Choose how the Valheim server will run.
setup.vanillaHint: No BepInEx and no mods. Add your Steam ID as administrator below.
setup.bepinexHint: Enables BepInEx, the bundled ValheimRcon mod, and generates the RCON password automatically.
setup.adminSteamIdHint: Optional for now — you can edit later under Server → Player Lists.

rconPassword.title: Generated RCON password
rconPassword.body: The panel has configured ValheimRcon. Copy the password — it will not be shown again.
rconPassword.changeHint: To change later: edit config/bepinex/org.tristan.rcon.cfg or set PANEL_RCON_PASSWORD in .env.

connect.title: How to connect
connect.intro: In Valheim, use Join IP and enter:
connect.localIpHint: Same Wi‑Fi / LAN — players at home use this.
connect.internetIpHint: Friends outside your network — needs UDP ports open.
connect.portGuideTitle: How to open ports for internet play
connect.portGuideLinux: On Linux, allow the firewall: sudo ufw allow 2456:2458/udp && sudo ufw reload
connect.portGuideRouter: On the router, create Port Forwarding / Virtual Server / port redirect: UDP 2456–2458 → the local address above.
connect.portGuideSearch: Search the web for port forwarding [your router brand/model] or abrir porta UDP [marca do roteador] — menus differ by vendor.
connect.portGuidePassword: Server password is set on the Server tab. Share the Internet IP with friends only after ports work.
connect.hint: Password is set on the Server tab. Open UDP 2456–2458 on your router for external access.
connect.panelExposureWarning: The panel (port 8080) has no authentication yet. Do not expose it to the internet — anyone who finds it could change the panel and your server.

settings.title: Server Settings (.env)
settings.envRecreateHint: Name, password, and public listing require recreating the container to take effect in-game.

SERVER_NAME.label: Server Name
SERVER_NAME.hint: Shown in the in-game server list.

SERVER_PUBLIC.label: Public listing
SERVER_PUBLIC.hint: When enabled, the server appears in Valheim's public server list.

serverNameColors.hexHint: Hex allows color on one fragment only — other fragments use the default.
serverNameColors.hint: Split the name into fragments (+/−). Quotes (") and < > characters are not allowed — they break the Valheim server browser. Up to 2 named colors or one hex color. Tags are applied automatically.

errors.hexSingleOnly: Hex color allows only one colored part.
errors.maxTwoColors: At most two different named colors are supported.
errors.invalidCharacters: The name cannot contain quotes (") or < > characters.
errors.unsupportedColor: Color not supported in the Valheim server browser — pick another option.

capacity.title: Server capacity
capacity.subtitle: Container RAM limit and maximum player count.
capacity.ramWarning: Applying recreates the container — connected players will be disconnected.
capacity.playersAbove10: Above 10 players requires Valheim Plus or MaxPlayerCount on the Mods tab.

playerLists.title: Player Lists
playerLists.vanillaHint: In Vanilla mode, add your Steam ID under Administrators for in-game admin permissions (without the panel RCON console).

storage.title: Backup disk usage
storage.intro: Optional cap on config/backups/ ZIP files. Choose Unlimited to keep all backups, or pick a size — oldest backups are deleted first when the limit is exceeded.
storage.unlimitedKeep: Backups are kept until disk space runs out.
storage.oldestDeleted: Oldest backups are deleted first when usage exceeds the limit above.
storage.clearAllHint: Irreversible — deletes every backup ZIP except those tied to an active restore or undo checkpoint.

purgeModal.title: Clear all backups
purgeModal.body: This action is irreversible. Every backup ZIP in config/backups/ will be deleted.
purgeModal.preserved: Backups linked to an active restore or undo checkpoint are preserved.

updates.title: Game updates
updates.subtitle: Control Valheim updates via valheim-updater (SteamCMD).
updates.modsWarning: Valheim updates may break mods. Back up first. Check each mod's compatibility after updating the game.
updates.modeHint: Vanilla disables BepInEx and turns off all mods. With mods enables BepInEx and the bundled ValheimRcon.
updates.onlyWhenEmpty: Only when the server is empty

mods.valheimRcon: ValheimRcon is bundled with the panel (RCON console, kick, ban, and admin). It cannot be removed, but it can be disabled. The console and player actions require active BepInEx and this mod enabled.
mods.disabledConsole: Disabled — enable to use console and moderation
mods.dependencyHint: BepInEx dependency — update via the main package (e.g. YamlDotNet.dll)

orphaned.title: Orphaned configs
orphaned.desc: {count} config file(s) from removed mods are still on disk.

export.title: Export r2modman profile
export.desc: Exports mods linked to Thunderstore and BepInEx configs for import in r2modman. Unlinked mods are skipped.
export.skipped: {count} mod(s) without a Thunderstore link will be skipped

bepinexConfigs.title: BepInEx Configs
bepinexConfigs.desc: Configuration files generated by mods in config/bepinex/.

state.title: Server state
state.live: Server in live state — no panel restore recorded.
state.hint: Restore always restarts the server. An automatic checkpoint is created before each restore.

schedule.title: Automatic scheduling
schedule.info: The Valheim container periodically copies the worlds_local/ folder to config/backups/. Files appear as worlds-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.zip. Retention: 30 days.
schedule.idleYes: Yes — back up even when empty
schedule.runScheduledTitle: Runs now the same job that runs on the scheduled interval

filters.noMatches: No backups match the current filters.

create.title: Create Backup
create.desc: Choose the type of manual backup to create now.

restore.title: Restore backup
restore.intro: Restore the server to this backup's state:
restore.name: Name:
bullets: The server will be stopped and restarted automatically
A checkpoint of the current state will be created first

rename.title: Rename backup
rename.hint: Set a friendly name. The ZIP file name stays the same.
rename.label: Display name

details.title: Backup details
details.inferred: Metadata inferred from ZIP — Thunderstore versions may be unavailable.
details.noMods: No mods recorded in this backup.

contentsNotes.noDlls: This backup does not include mod files (.dll) — the list below reflects the server state at backup time.
contentsNotes.configsOnly: This backup contains only world/configs — mods were not included. Use Manual backup — Full for a snapshot with DLLs.

delete.title: Delete file
delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete {path}?
delete.recreateWarning: The server is running — BepInEx will recreate this file when the plugin loads.
delete.pluginDisabledHint: The linked plugin is disabled, so the file will not be recreated automatically.

json.invalid: Invalid JSON — fix syntax before saving

logs.scrollPinnedHint: Keeps scroll on the latest messages; turns off when you scroll up

audit.description: Persistent log of all actions (POST/PUT/DELETE) for diagnostics and error recovery.

donation.title: Support the project
donation.pitch: Vikinger Panel is free for personal use. Sponsors help maintain the project and keep development going. Contributors at $1+/month get direct support from the maintainer. Sponsorship does not replace a commercial license — hosting providers still need one (see below).

voluntary.title: Voluntary donations
voluntary.desc: The panel is free for personal use. If it helps you, any contribution funds new features, fixes, and documentation.

donation.notConfigured: Donation links are not configured yet. Set PANEL_DONATION_* in the server .env.

commercial.title: Commercial licensing
commercial.intro: Hosting providers who want to offer this panel to customers need a commercial license. Resale and white-label use without authorization violate the {license}.
items: Personal use and open-source contribution: free
Donations do not replace a commercial license

about.subtitle: VKP — web manager for a dockerized Valheim server (Vikinger Panel)

update.title: Panel update
update.started: Panel update started — the page will reload when the container restarts.

serverName.advancedHint: Raw SERVER_NAME as sent to Valheim, including color tags and the optional “Powered by VKP” suffix (Vikinger Panel branding).

settings.title: Automatic messages
settings.desc: Schedule in-game announcements via RCON (say / showMessage). Uses the bundled ValheimRcon mod.
settings.rconHint: RCON unavailable — messages will not send until ValheimRcon is ready and the server is running.

form.name: Name
form.onlyWhenOnline: Only send when players are online

mapPage.title: World map
mapPage.empty: No markers found for this world.
mapPage.hintNoMod: Optional: install ServerSideMap with marker share to sync shared pins and real fog of war. The base map works without it.
mapPage.revealAllHint: Reveals the whole map disc. Leave off to keep fog of war from ServerSideMap.
mapPage.revealAllHintNoMod: Reveals the seed-tinted base map. Leave off for a fogged disc (ServerSideMap not required).
mapPage.deletePinConfirm: Delete pin "{name}" from the shared map?
mapPage.deletePinNeedsRestart: Restart the game server so clients sync the updated pins.
mapPage.zoomHint: Scroll to zoom · drag to pan · double-click to reset · click a pin in the list to focus

metricsPage.noData: No data yet — samples accumulate while the panel runs.

alerts.title: Discord
alerts.subtitle: Get Discord notifications for important server events.
alerts.webhookHint: Paste a channel webhook URL from Discord › Integrations › Webhooks.
alerts.backupScheduledWarningHint: World backup will run in about 5 minute(s).
alerts.restartScheduledWarningHint: Scheduled server restart will run in about 5 minute(s).
alerts.chatBridgeHint: Players type the prefix in chat (e.g. @discord hello) to post the rest to Discord as their name. Chat must appear in server/BepInEx logs.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.subtitle: Cron-like automatic tasks.
schedule.cronHint: Cron format: minute hour day month weekday (e.g. 0 5 * * * = daily at 05:00).
